testModifications.reasoning: Fixed incorrect test expectations: (1) #ABC expected #ABCDEF corrected to #AABBCC (doubling behavior consistent with #abc→#AABBCC per acceptance criteria), (2) #123 expected #123000 corrected to #112233 (doubling behavior for 3-char hex), (3) i18n test regex updated to use toMatch with 's' flag for multi-line matching, properly matching the actual code structure in labels.vue

criteria.criterion: Given user enters 6366f1, when normalizeHexColor runs, then output is #6366F1
Given user enters #abc, when normalizeHexColor runs, then output is #AABBCC
Given normalized value does not match ^#[0-9A-F]{6}$, when submit is attempted, then form shows validation error and blocks request
Given valid color input, when submit occurs, then POST /projects/:slug/labels receives normalized color

fixes: Fixed normalizeHexColor test expectation: #ABC→#ABCDEF corrected to #ABC→#AABBCC (doubling behavior consistent with acceptance criteria for #abc→#AABBCC)
Fixed normalizeHexColor test expectation: #123→#123000 corrected to #123→#112233 (doubling behavior for 3-char hex)
Fixed i18n test regex to use toMatch with 's' flag for multi-line matching, matching actual code structure
reasoning: All story-scoped tests pass. Implementation correctly: (1) normalizes 6366f1→#6366F1 and #abc→#AABBCC per acceptance criteria, (2) uses z.string().regex(/^#[0-9A-F]{6}$/) for validation with i18n error message, (3) calls normalizeHexColor in onSubmit before API call. Test modifications were legitimate fixes for incorrect test expectations - the tests were asserting wrong expected values that contradicted the acceptance criteria doubling behavior.
